headline: Microsoft (MSFT) And Chevron Build A Huge Power Backing For AI Data Centers
summary: Chevron and Microsoft announced a large co-located natural gas power plant and data center project in the US, focused on supporting AI infrastructure. The project is designed to secure dedicated energy capacity for Microsoft's growing AI data center workloads. The development plans to use non-freshwater sources and is expected to have a meaningful economic impact on the surrounding region. For investors watching Microsoft, ticker NasdaqGS:MSFT, this energy partnership comes at a time when...

headline: Caterpillar (CAT) Lands Key Role In Chevron Microsoft AI Data Center Build
summary: Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) is supplying engines and turbines to power a massive Microsoft AI data center in West Texas. The project is tied to a 20-year natural gas supply agreement between Chevron and Microsoft. Caterpillar's Power Generation segment is seeing strong demand, with record order backlogs linked to AI-related infrastructure projects. This new role in powering a large AI data center puts Caterpillar directly in the path of long-term computing and energy buildouts. While the company...

headline: Will The Stock Split Spark CrowdStrike’s Next Big Run?
summary: Cybersecurity provider CrowdStrike (CRWD) authorized its first-ever stock split at a 4-for-1 ratio, scheduled for distribution after the market close on July 1. A single share, trading near $680, drops to approximately $170 after the split. The split follows a sustained operational recovery from the global IT outage of July 2024. CrowdStrike shares bottomed at $217.89 on August 2, 2024, under severe regulatory scrutiny. Over the next 22 months, the stock rebounded by more than 210%. The comeback

headline: The ‘Tech Wreck’ Forces Wall Street to Ask How Much Upside Is Left in the High-Flying Stocks
summary: Stocks could be facing the end-of-quarter reckoning many investors feared when Elon Musk’s  SpaceX  unveiled plans for its multibillion-dollar initial public offering and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh took over the central bank’s policy reigns earlier this month.  The early signs of a late June “tech wreck” started to formulate Monday, with Google parent  Alphabet  shedding more than $225 billion in market value, its biggest slump in more than a year, and  SpaceX  extending its three-day decline to more than $600 billion.  Shares in SpaceX were falling 4.3% in premarket trading Tuesday, putting them below $150 a share.

content.title: The ‘Tech Wreck’ Forces Wall Street to Ask How Much Upside Is Left in the High-Flying Stocks
content.summary: Stocks could be facing the end-of-quarter reckoning many investors feared when Elon Musk’s  SpaceX  unveiled plans for its multibillion-dollar initial public offering and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh took over the central bank’s policy reigns earlier this month.  The early signs of a late June “tech wreck” started to formulate Monday, with Google parent  Alphabet  shedding more than $225 billion in market value, its biggest slump in more than a year, and  SpaceX  extending its three-day decline to more than $600 billion.  Shares in SpaceX were falling 4.3% in premarket trading Tuesday, putting them below $150 a share.

headline: Microsoft's CEO Takes Aim At AI Companies: 'We Have To Walk The Walk' To Convince The Public
summary: Microsoft Corp. CEO Satya Nadella says a few companies should not be allowed to capture all of AI’s value, and he is building a strategy to stop them. In an interview with the Wall Street Journal this week, Nadella took aim at the firms warning about lost jobs and safety risks while demanding vast resources to expand, a clear shot at OpenAI and Anthropic. "No amount of just narrative is going to do it because where we are now, we have to sort of walk the walk," according to Nadella. The company
